Add priority field to tasks, decisions, and questions

EOF
)"

content.text: Now I'll create the PR on GitHub:

input.command:
gh pr create --base main --title "Add priority field to tasks, decisions, and questions" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
Implements priority extraction and persistence across the extraction pipeline, database schema, entity APIs, and UI components. The priority field (critical, high, medium, low) is inferred from conversation text urgency signals like "ASAP", "urgent", "eventually", and "nice to have".

## Changes
- Schema: Added priority field to task, decision, and question tables
- Extraction: Updated prompt and schema to extract priority; added priority accuracy scorer  
- Persistence: Integrated priority handling in entity-upsert and persist-extraction pipelines
- API: Extended entity detail and work item acceptance endpoints to return/persist priority
- UI: Enhanced EntityDetailPanel and work item suggestion components to display priority badges
- Tests: Added smoke tests for end-to-end priority validation and eval fixtures

## Test Coverage
All changes validated via updated extraction evals, golden cases, smoke tests, and priority accuracy scorer.
EOF
)"
input.description: Create pull request on main branch

content.thinking: Perfect! The PR has been created. Let me provide the user with a summary of what was accomplished.

content.text:
✅ **PR Created Successfully!**

**PR #57:** [Add priority field to tasks, decisions, and questions](https://github.com/marcus-sa/brain/pull/57)

### Summary of Changes
- **16 files modified, 2 new files** (442 insertions, 49 deletions)
- Added priority field extraction and persistence across the pipeline
- Updated schema, contracts, and types to support priority (critical, high, medium, low)
- Enhanced UI to display priority in entity detail panels and work item suggestions
- Added smoke tests and priority accuracy eval scorer for validation
- Integrated priority handling in extraction prompt, persistence, and API endpoints

The PR is now ready for review!
